columns.extraction_plan: Extract the NCT number 'NCT00268476' from the 'Methods' section on page 1, which states 'STAMPEDE is registered with ClinicalTrials.gov (NCT00268476) and ISRCTN (ISRCTN78818544)'.
The PubMed ID is not reported within the provided PDF content. It would typically be assigned by PubMed upon indexing the article.

Extract the main title of the article on page 1: 'Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone with or without enzalutamide for patients with metastatic prostate cancer starting androgen deprivation therapy: final results from two randomised phase 3 trials of the STAMPEDE platform protocol'.
Extract the list of authors from the author byline on page 1, starting with 'Gerhardt Attard' and ending with 'Nicholas D James†, for the STAMPEDE investigators‡'.
Extract the year '2023' from the journal citation 'Lancet Oncol 2023' at the top right of page 1 or from the footer 'Vol 24 May 2023' on any page.
The document is a full publication, as indicated by its comprehensive structure, multiple sections (Summary, Methods, Findings, Interpretation, etc.), and length across several pages.
Extract 'phase 3' from the title of the article on page 1: 'final results from two randomised phase 3 trials'.

The title on page 1 states 'final results' and the 'Summary' section on page 1 mentions 'evaluate long-term outcomes'. The 'Introduction' on page 2 further clarifies 'The STAMPEDE abiraterone trial has now closed, 10 years after initiation of accrual, providing the opportunity to assess new data on long-term efficacy', indicating this is a follow-up publication.
